title: System vs user prompt placement
hypothesis: Restructuring prompt layers optimizes output quality

A_baseline.description: Full system prompt (preamble + quality criteria + evidence rules + example); cluster data in user message

B_all_in_user.description: Minimal system prompt (role declaration only); all instructions + evidence rules + cluster data in user message

C_instructions_split.description: System prompt contains preamble + quality criteria only (no evidence rules, no example); user message contains cluster data + inline evidence reminder

target_metric.name: quality_delta_per_layout
target_metric.note: All layouts tied at ceiling (1.0) — no signal

rationale: All three prompt layouts produced perfect groundedness (1.0) on both clusters. The delta across layouts is 0.0, placing this firmly in NOISE territory. The baseline Layout A (full system prompt) is already optimal for this metric and dataset. A definitive quality comparison would require a harder dataset where at least one layout falls below the 0.9 threshold, or a semantic quality metric beyond structural groundedness.
